# case189_public_function_private_parameter_type — Public function parameter retyped to an internal type

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Verdict** | 🔴 **BREAKING** |
| **Category** | Breaking |
| **Platforms** | Linux |
| **Flags** | ABI break, API break, Bad practice |
| **Detected `ChangeKind`s** | `func_removed`, `public_api_internal_dependency_added` |
| **Source files** | `examples/case189_public_function_private_parameter_type/` |
| **Known kind gap** | `public_api_internal_dependency_added` — verdict is correct; see note below |

**Verdict:** 🔴 BREAKING · **Findings:** `func_removed` (artifact-proven) + `public_api_internal_dependency_added` (L5, correlated) · **Evidence tier:** L1 for the verdict; L5 (`--header-graph`) for the risk finding

This is a real, compiled `v1`/`v2` example, verified end-to-end against gcc
and clang.

## What it demonstrates

`demo::configure()`'s opaque `void*` parameter is retyped to
`detail::Options*` — a type declared only in an internal, non-public header
(`detail_private.h`, never passed as `--public-header`/`-H`).

Changing a parameter's type changes the function's mangled name in C++: the
old exported symbol (`configure(void*, Meta)`) genuinely disappears and a
different one (`configure(detail::Options*, Meta)`) appears. abicheck
correctly reports it as BREAKING via `func_removed` — no build integration
needed, the default `debug-headers` lane catches this. The **Real Failure
Demo** below shows the concrete loader failure this causes.

Layered on top, `dump --header-graph` additionally proves, via the L5 source
graph, that `demo::configure` newly carries a `DECL_HAS_TYPE` edge to
`demo::detail::Options` — a dependency it did not have in v1 — and reports
`public_api_internal_dependency_added`. This is *correlated context* on an
already-detected break, not a standalone signal: it names exactly which
internal type the public function's new signature now depends on.

### Why this isn't the original "invisible below L5" scenario

An earlier version of this catalog modeled this case as a hand-built L5
graph fixture, with the premise that `public_api_internal_dependency_added`
would be the *only* finding — verdict `COMPATIBLE_WITH_RISK`, invisible to
every lower evidence tier. That premise was never verified against a real
compiled example, and it does not hold: any real parameter-type change a
person can see in the header changes the mangled symbol, which abicheck's
existing symbol-presence detector already catches. Only a *body-only*
reference (no signature change at all — see
`case190`)
can stay genuinely invisible outside L5.

## How to reproduce

```bash
g++ -std=c++17 -shared -fPIC -g v1.cpp -o libv1.so
g++ -std=c++17 -shared -fPIC -g v2.cpp -o libv2.so

# Default debug-headers lane: BREAKING, via func_removed alone.
python3 -m abicheck.cli dump libv1.so --header v1.h -o v1.json
python3 -m abicheck.cli dump libv2.so --header v2.h -o v2.json
python3 -m abicheck.cli compare v1.json v2.json
# → BREAKING: func_removed (configure)

# With --header-graph: the same BREAKING verdict, plus the L5 risk finding.
python3 -m abicheck.cli dump libv1.so --header v1.h --public-header v1.h --header-graph -o v1.json
python3 -m abicheck.cli dump libv2.so --header v2.h --public-header v2.h --header-graph -o v2.json
python3 -m abicheck.cli compare v1.json v2.json
# → BREAKING: func_removed, public_api_internal_dependency_added
#   Proof path: configure --[DECL_HAS_TYPE]--> demo::detail::Options
```

## Real Failure Demo

**Severity: 🔴 CRITICAL — hard loader failure**

```bash
cmake -S examples -B /tmp/abicheck-examples-build -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Debug
cmake --build /tmp/abicheck-examples-build --target case189_public_function_private_parameter_type_app case189_public_function_private_parameter_type_v2

tmp=$(mktemp -d)
cp /tmp/abicheck-examples-build/case189_public_function_private_parameter_type/app_v1 "$tmp/"
cp /tmp/abicheck-examples-build/case189_public_function_private_parameter_type/libv2.so "$tmp/libv1.so"
(cd "$tmp" && LD_LIBRARY_PATH=. ./app_v1)
# ./app_v1: symbol lookup error: ./app_v1: undefined symbol: _ZN4demo9configureEPvNS_4MetaE
```

## Sibling cases

- `case160_public_api_internal_dep_added` — same finding family via a `DECL_CALLS_DECL` edge (a public function calling an internal one), hand-built fixture.
- `case187_public_struct_private_field_type` — same finding family via `TYPE_HAS_FIELD_TYPE` (a private field type), real compiled example.
- `case188_public_class_private_base_class` — same finding family via `TYPE_INHERITS` (a private base class), real compiled example.

## How to fix

Either promote `detail::Options` to a documented, stable part of the public
API, or take/return only public types (e.g. a builder pattern, or an opaque
public handle that wraps the internal type).

---

## Ground-truth provenance

**Known kind gap:** public_api_internal_dependency_added is only emitted by `dump --header-graph`, an opt-in flag tests/validate_examples.py's default gcc/clang debug-headers lane does not pass (see tests/validate_examples.py's _kinds_strict_signal call site). The BREAKING verdict is still correct via func_removed alone; --header-graph is exercised for real, separately, by tests/test_header_graph_examples.py (wired into the full-matrix proof gate via the header_graph OWNER_PROOFS/SPECIAL_PROOFS entry in validation/scripts/run_example_owner_proofs.py and collect_full_example_matrix.py).
